title: tragopogon crocifolius subsp. nebrodensis
intro: Tragopogon crocifolius subsp. nebrodensis, commonly known as Sulla, is a plant species in the sunflower family, characterized by its yellow or white flowers and edible taproot. Traditionally, the roots of Sulla have been used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other compounds. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, with its health benefits thought to be due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Sulla is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed raw or cooked as a vegetable, although its edibility and safety should be confirmed through proper identification and preparation.
